explanations.en: This action ranks first because it is expected to cut emissions strongly, has very strong supportive policy context, and looks feasible for the city to carry out. The only limitation noted is that no legal assessment row was available, so the legal part used a neutral fallback.

This action is close behind because it has the same strong emissions reduction, supportive policy context, and strong feasibility as the top-ranked option. It sits slightly lower without any additional strengths listed to push it ahead.

This action ranks well because it can deliver emissions benefits on a very favorable timeline and also has very strong policy support and feasibility. It does not rank higher because its expected emissions reduction is limited in the current city inventory.

This action stays near the top because it has very strong policy support and very strong feasibility for the city. It ranks below the first three because its expected emissions reduction is limited in the current city inventory.

This action is supported by strong policy context and very strong feasibility, which keeps it in the upper half of the list. It ranks lower because its expected emissions reduction is limited in the current city inventory.

This action has very strong policy support and strong feasibility, which helps it stay in the middle of the ranking. It does not place higher because its expected emissions reduction is limited in the current city inventory.

This action benefits from a very favorable timeline, strong policy support, and very strong feasibility. It remains below the top group because its expected emissions reduction is limited in the current city inventory.

This action can deliver emissions benefits on a very favorable timeline and also has strong policy support and very strong feasibility. It ranks lower because its expected emissions reduction is limited in the current city inventory.

This action has a very favorable timeline, strong policy support, and very strong feasibility, which keeps it in the lower middle of the ranking. Its main drawback is that its expected emissions reduction is limited in the current city inventory.

This action is helped by a very favorable timeline, strong policy support, and very strong feasibility. It stays below similar actions because its expected emissions reduction is limited in the current city inventory.
This action has strong policy support and strong feasibility, but its expected emissions reduction is limited in the current city inventory. That keeps it below the stronger-impact options.
This action has strong policy support and strong feasibility, but its expected emissions reduction is limited in the current city inventory. That keeps it in the lower half of the ranking.
This action stands out for very strong policy support and very strong feasibility. It ranks lower because its expected emissions reduction is limited in the current city inventory.

This action has very strong policy support and strong feasibility, which helps it remain on the list. It does not rank higher because its expected emissions reduction is limited in the current city inventory.

This action has strong policy support, but its expected emissions reduction is limited in the current city inventory. It also has a neutral fallback for feasibility because no mitigation feasibility score row was available.
This action has strong expected emissions reduction and strong policy support, but its benefits arrive on a slow timeline. It also has weaker feasibility for the city, which holds it down.

This action has strong policy support, but its expected emissions reduction is limited in the current city inventory. With no legal assessment row available, the legal part also used a neutral fallback.

This action can deliver emissions benefits on a very favorable timeline and has strong policy support. It ranks low because its expected emissions reduction is limited in the current city inventory, and feasibility used a neutral fallback where no score row was available.
This action has strong policy support and very strong feasibility, but its expected emissions reduction is limited in the current city inventory. That keeps it below the higher-ranked options.
